chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي كَمْ يُقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنُ وَ يُخْتَمُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – In how much (time) should the Quran be recited and completed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/504)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/504)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ فِي كَمْ أَقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنَ فَقَالَ اقْرَأْهُ أَخْمَاساً اقْرَأْهُ أَسْبَاعاً أَمَا إِنَّ عِنْدِي مُصْحَفاً مُجَزًّى أَرْبَعَةَ عَشَرَ جُزْءاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhamad Bin Al Husayn, from Ali Bin Al Nu’man, from Yaqoub Bin Shuayb, from Husayn Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘In how much (time) should I recite the (whole) Quran?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Recite it in fifths or sevenths. But, with me<sup>asws</sup> there is a Parchment sectioned upon fourteen parts’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Ali ibn al-Nu’Man from Ya’qub ibn Shu’ayb from Husayn ibn Khalid who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘In how many days should one complete reading the Holy Quran?’ The Imam said, ‘You may read it in five or seven days. I, however, have a copy of the Holy Quran that is divided into fourteen parts.’”
